name: PELVIC CONGESTION SYNDROME
definition: chronic (> 6 months) noncyclical pelvic pain associated with pelvic venous distension + obstructive uropathy ← ureteral compression at pelvic brim by dilated / aberrant /

demographics.Prevalence: ovarian varices in 10% in general population
demographics.Location: around uterus, ovary, broad ligament, pelvic sidewall, paravaginal plexus

dominant_finding: multiple tortuous pelvic veins > 4-6 mm in diameter around uterus + ovaries
distribution: around uterus, ovary, broad ligament, pelvic sidewall, paravaginal plexus
discriminating_features: medial deviation of obstructed segment (with retrocaval ureter) = characteristic
multiple tortuous pelvic veins > 4-6 mm in diameter around uterus + ovaries

US: multiple tortuous pelvic veins > 4-6 mm in diameter around uterus + ovaries
retrograde flow in ovarian / pelvic veins
delayed / stagnant clearance of injected contrast from veins
medial deviation of obstructed segment (with retrocaval ureter) = characteristic
concurrent evaluation of cause of compression, implicated vessel + proximal dilatation

clinical.Rx: hormone analogues (medroxyprogestrone, GnRH analog goserelin) + analgesics;
